ddls:
CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the companies table */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the product_types table */,
  "screen_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the screens table */,
  "cpu_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the cpus table */,
  "ram_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the rams table */,
  "storage_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the storages table */,
  "gpu_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the gpus table */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the specific model */,
  "sku" TEXT /* Stock Keeping Unit for inventory management */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date the product was released */,
  "warranty_period" INTEGER /* Warranty period in months */,
  "battery_life" REAL /* Estimated battery life in hours */,
  "color" TEXT /* Color of the product */,
  "availability_status" TEXT /* Current availability status (e.g., In Stock, Out of Stock) */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Last time the product information was updated */,
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the vendors table */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the product */,
  "weight" REAL /* Weight of the product in pounds */,
  "x_res" INTEGER /* Horizontal resolution */,
  "y_res" INTEGER /* Vertical resolution */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_company_id FOREIGN KEY ("company_id") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_cpu_id FOREIGN KEY ("cpu_id") REFERENCES cpus ("cpu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_ram_id FOREIGN KEY ("ram_id") REFERENCES rams ("ram_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_vendor_id FOREIGN KEY ("vendor_id") REFERENCES vendors ("vendor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_storage_id FOREIGN KEY ("storage_id") REFERENCES storages ("storage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_gpu_id FOREIGN KEY ("gpu_id") REFERENCES gpus ("gpu_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES product_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_products_screen_id FOREIGN KEY ("screen_id") REFERENCES screens ("screen_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the company */,
  "address" TEXT /* Company's address */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Company's phone number */,
  "website" TEXT /* Company's website URL */,
  "founded_year" INTEGER /* Year the company was founded */,
  "ceo" TEXT /* Name of the CEO */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the company's headquarters */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the product type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product type (e.g., Consumer, Enterprise) */,
  "target_market" TEXT /* Target market for the product type (e.g., Students, Professionals) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "screens" (
  "screen_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each screen */,
  "screen_size" REAL /* Screen size in inches */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Screen resolution */,
  "ips" INTEGER /* Whether the screen is IPS (0 for no, 1 for yes) */,
  "touchscreen" INTEGER /* Whether the screen is touchscreen (0 for no, 1 for yes) */,
  "refresh_rate" INTEGER /* Screen refresh rate in Hz */,
  "panel_type" TEXT /* Type of panel (e.g., VA, TN) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("screen_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpus" (
  "cpu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CPU */,
  "cpu_name" TEXT /* Name of the CPU */,
  "speed" TEXT /* Speed of the CPU */,
  "cores" INTEGER /* Number of cores */,
  "threads" INTEGER /* Number of threads */,
  "cache_size" TEXT /* Size of the cache (e.g., 8MB L3) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cpu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "rams" (
  "ram_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each RAM */,
  "ram_size" INTEGER /* Size of the RAM in GB */,
  "speed" TEXT /* Speed of the RAM (e.g., 3200 MHz) */,
  "type" TEXT /* Type of RAM (e.g., DDR4) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("ram_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "storages" (
  "storage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each storage device */,
  "storage_type" TEXT /* Type of the storage device (e.g., SSD, HDD) */,
  "storage_size" TEXT /* Size of the storage device */,
  "read_speed" TEXT /* Read speed of the storage (e.g., 3500 MB/s) */,
  "write_speed" TEXT /* Write speed of the storage (e.g., 3000 MB/s) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("storage_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "gpus" (
  "gpu_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each GPU */,
  "gpu_name" TEXT /* Name of the GPU */,
  "vram_size" TEXT /* Size of the VRAM (e.g., 4GB GDDR6) */,
  "vram_type" TEXT /* Type of VRAM (e.g., GDDR6) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("gpu_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vendors" (
  "vendor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each vendor */,
  "vendor_name" TEXT /* Name of the vendor */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the vendor */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the vendor */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the vendor */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vendor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales" (
  "sale_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sale */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the products table */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity sold */,
  "sale_date" TEXT /* Date of the sale */,
  "total_price" REAL /* Total price of the sale */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the customers table */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sale_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the customer */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the customer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for login */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., Sales, Inventory, Admin) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last time the user logged in */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active (0 for no, 1 for yes) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)
